footer.license: Includes icons from Remix Icon (MIT License)

home.title: Welcome to NUI
home.subtitle: A modern, customizable Angular component library
home.description: Build beautiful applications with our complete set of UI components

multitheme.title: Powerful theme system
multitheme.description: Customize the look of your application with multiple predefined presets and support for custom themes.

darkMode.title: Smart dark mode
darkMode.description: Built-in dark mode support with manual, automatic, and system-based strategies.

components.title: Versatile components
components.description: A wide range of ready-to-use UI components, designed to be accessible and easy to customize.

installation.title: Installation
installation.subtitle: Get started with NUI in your Angular project
installation.step1Description: Use npm or yarn to install the NUI package in your project.
installation.step2Description: Configure NUI in your application configuration file. Define the preset you want to use and the theme mode.
installation.step3Description: You can import NUI styles in three different ways: in your global styles.scss file, in angular.json, or in project.json if you use Nx.
installation.step4Description: You can now use any NUI component in your HTML templates.

configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.subtitle: Configure NUI to suit your needs

presets.title: Preset configuration
presets.description: NUI includes 12 predefined presets you can use directly in your application. Each preset defines a unique color palette for light and dark modes, ensuring a consistent look across all components.
presets.note: You can change the preset at runtime using the ThemeService, allowing you to offer your users the option to choose their preferred appearance.

custom-presets.title: Custom presets
custom-presets.description: In addition to the predefined presets, you can create your own custom presets by defining specific colors for light and dark modes. This allows you to tailor your application's appearance to your brand identity or create unique user experiences.
custom-presets.note: Custom presets are defined as TypeScript objects that conform to the ThemePreset interface, ensuring type-safe configuration and seamless integration with NUI's theming system.

dark-mode.title: Dark mode configuration
dark-mode.description: Control how dark mode is applied in your application. Available options are: manual (controlled by code), auto (based on time of day), or system (based on the operating system's preferences).
dark-mode.note: Dark mode can be activated programmatically using the ThemeService, allowing you to offer users the option to toggle between modes or automatically follow their system settings.

component-config.title: Component-specific configuration
component-config.description: NUI allows configuring certain aspects of components through global configuration, such as the Paginator's 'Shapeshifter' pagination system or the available variants for the Button. This gives you control over component behavior and appearance without needing to modify source code.
component-config.note: By configuring component-specific options in the NUI provider, you can ensure a consistent user experience throughout your application and adapt components to your needs effortlessly.

i18n.title: Internationalization (i18n) configuration
i18n.description:
NUI implements a reactive i18n system based on Signals. You can set an initial text base using the provideNuiI18n() provider. For multi-language applications, the NuiI18nService allows injecting new dictionaries at runtime via the setLang() method. When doing so, all subscribed components update automatically without reloading the application or third-party dependencies.

Use setTranslations to update only a subset of texts by specifying the dictionary root. For example, if you use ngx-translate, you can indicate where the 'NUI' json object is located within your translations file and pass that object to the NUI service to keep everything in sync.
i18n.note: Texts are dynamically updated via the service, while date locales must be pre-registered in the global configuration so the adapter can reactively process formats and ARIA labels.

i18n-date.title: NUI i18n in dates
i18n-date.description:
For components like Calendar to handle formats and accessibility labels (ARIA) in different languages, you must register locale objects (e.g., date-fns) using provideNuiDateLocales().

NUI automatically synchronizes these locales with the i18n service, ensuring that both texts and dates (e.g., "Monday, January 1") are grammatically correct and accessible without additional configuration.
i18n-date.note: When you change the application language via nuiI18nService.setLang('en'), NUI automatically syncs the Date Adapter and Text Dictionary. The entire system is reactive: your calendars and paginators will update instantly without extra logic in your components.

theming.title: Themes & presets
theming.subtitle: Customize the look of your application
theming.description: NUI includes multiple predefined presets. Each preset defines a unique color palette applied to all components. Switch presets at runtime to see changes instantly. Additionally, switching between light and dark mode automatically adjusts colors to maintain contrast and readability.
theming.selectPreset: Select a preset to preview it

presetDescriptions.aura: Modern and elegant default palette with turquoise and violet tones
presetDescriptions.autumn: Warm and inviting palette with coral and golden tones, perfect for e-commerce
presetDescriptions.neon: Futuristic cyberpunk palette with electric cyan and neon colors, ideal for gaming
presetDescriptions.dopamine: Optimistic and cheerful palette with bright lime and pink tones
presetDescriptions.corporate: Professional blue palette, perfect for business applications
presetDescriptions.minimal: Refined minimalist palette with subtle colors and elegant purple accent
presetDescriptions.sunset: Warm and vibrant palette with pink, orange and coral tones, perfect for creative apps
presetDescriptions.twilight: Soft and relaxing palette with lavender and grayish-blue tones, ideal for wellness
presetDescriptions.ocean: Calm and professional palette with deep blues and water tones, ideal for productivity
presetDescriptions.emerald: Fresh and natural palette with emerald greens and turquoise accents, perfect for health and nature apps
presetDescriptions.coffee: Warm and cozy palette with brown tones and cream accents, ideal for lifestyle and food apps
presetDescriptions.velvet: Rich and luxurious palette with burgundy tones and rosy accents, perfect for elegant and sophisticated apps

overview.title: Theming system
overview.subtitle: A powerful, runtime-configurable theming solution for Angular applications

whatItSolves.title: What problem does it solve?
whatItSolves.description: NUI's theming system eliminates the need for manual CSS configuration, theme rebuilding, or complex SCSS variable management. It provides a unified, type-safe way to customize your application's appearance at runtime.

runtimeSwitching.title: Real-time theme switching
runtimeSwitching.description: Switch themes instantly without reloading the page or recompiling. Perfect for user preferences and A/B testing.

darkMode.title: Automatic dark mode
darkMode.description: Built-in dark mode support with automatic color adjustments and multiple activation strategies.

tokenGeneration.title: 200+ CSS variables
tokenGeneration.description: Comprehensive variable system covering colors, shadows, tints, alpha values and component-specific tokens.

componentSpecific.title: Component-specific variables
componentSpecific.description: Each component has its own customizable CSS variables that inherit from the global theme.

typeSafety.title: Type-safe configuration
typeSafety.description: Full TypeScript support with interfaces that ensure your custom themes are correctly configured.

zeroScss.title: No build overhead
zeroScss.description: Themes are generated in the browser at runtime. No need to recompile the application when switching themes.

integration.title: How it integrates
integration.description: The theming system is deeply integrated into NUI and works seamlessly across all components:
integration.point1: Single configuration point: Define your theme once in provideNUI()
integration.point2: Automatic propagation: All NUI components automatically consume theme variables
integration.point3: Custom components: Your own components can use NUI CSS variables for consistent styles
integration.point4: Runtime control: Change themes or activate dark mode programmatically via ThemeService
integration.point5: Framework-agnostic CSS: Variables work in any HTML/CSS, not just Angular components

keyFeatures.title: Key technical features
keyFeatures.feat1: CSS variables dynamically generated from TypeScript configuration
keyFeatures.feat2: (primary, secondary, accent, success, info, warning, danger, neutral)
keyFeatures.feat3: for consistent neutrals in light and dark modes
keyFeatures.feat4: (shadows, tints, alpha values) generated for each color
keyFeatures.feat5: with manual, system, and time-based strategies

architecture.title: Theming architecture
architecture.subtitle: A layered system for dynamic CSS variable generation
architecture.overview: NUI's theming system uses a layered architecture that dynamically generates more than 1000 CSS variables from the preset configuration. Each layer builds on the previous one, creating a cohesive and scalable system.

layer1.title: Layer 1: Preset system (semantic colors)
layer1.description: Each preset defines 8 semantic colors that are applied consistently throughout the application. These colors automatically adapt their tone between light and dark modes.
layer1.primary: Main brand color, primary actions and key navigation elements
layer1.secondary: Complementary color for visual hierarchy and secondary actions
layer1.accent: Emphasis color for highlights, links and interactive states
layer1.success: Positive confirmations, successful operations and correct validation
layer1.info: Informational messages, neutral notifications and help content
layer1.warning: Caution alerts that require attention but are not critical
layer1.danger: Critical errors, destructive actions and failed validation
layer1.neutral: Neutral elements, disabled states and subtle backgrounds

layer2.title: Layer 2: Structural variables
layer2.description: Base variables that define text colors, backgrounds, borders and other structural elements. Generated from the preset's grayscale and adapted to light/dark mode.

surfaces.title: Surfaces
surfaces.surface: Main background for cards, modals and panels
surfaces.surfaceNeutral: Neutral background for disabled states or placeholders

text.title: Text
text.secondary: Secondary text for metadata and labels

layer3.title: Layer 3: Variant generation
layer3.description: For each semantic color, the system automatically generates tint, shade and alpha variants. This allows visual hierarchy without defining additional colors.

tints.title: Tints (Lighter)
tints.description: Lightened versions by blending the color with white. Ideal for soft backgrounds, hover states and subtle accents.

shades.title: Shades (Darker)
shades.description: Darkened versions by blending the color with black. Used for active states, borders and visual depth.

alpha.title: Alpha values (Transparency)
alpha.description: Semi-transparent versions of the color. Perfect for overlays, glass effects and disabled states.

layer4.title: Layer 4: Shadow system
layer4.description: Two-level shadow system: primitives (xs, sm, md, lg, xl) and semantic aliases that map use cases to primitives.

primitives.xs: Minimal shadow for inputs and form fields
primitives.sm: Subtle shadow for cards and containers
primitives.lg: Elevated shadow for popovers and dropdowns
primitives.xl: Maximum shadow for modals and overlays

layer5.title: Layer 5: Component variables
layer5.description: Each component has a set of dynamically generated CSS variables derived from semantic colors and adapted to variants (solid, outline, ghost).

button.description: Variables for 3 variants (Solid, Outline, Ghost) × 8 colors × 4 states (default, hover, active, disabled). Total: ~96 variables.

toast.description: Specific variables for 4 toast types (success, info, warning, danger) including background, border, title, text, progress and icon.

paginator.description: 'Shapeshifter' system where inactive buttons are ghost and the active button adopts the specified variant. Navigation buttons are always ghost.

performance.title: Performance optimization
performance.description: The theming system includes optimizations to minimize the performance impact of dynamic theme switching.

cache.title: CSS cache
cache.description: The generated CSS for each preset+mode combination is cached in memory. Subsequent changes are instant by reusing the pre-generated CSS.

pregeneration.title: Idle pre-generation
pregeneration.description: On app load, CSS for both modes (light/dark) is pre-generated using requestIdleCallback. The first toggle is already instant.

containment.title: CSS Containment
containment.description: Heavy components use 'contain: layout style paint' to limit the scope of style recalculations.

flow.title: Variable generation flow
flow.description: From preset selection to DOM application, here's how the process works:

step1.title: User selects preset or toggles dark mode

step2.title: ThemeService checks CSS cache
step2.description: Key 'presetName-isDarkMode' is generated. If it exists in cache, it's reused. Otherwise, it's generated.

step3.title: CSS variable generation
step3.description: ~1000 color calculations (tints, shades, alphas, contrast) are executed to create the complete CSS string.

step4.title: DOM injection
step4.description: CSS is injected into <style id='nui-theme-colors'>. The browser recalculates styles and re-renders affected components.

darkMode.title: Dark Mode Strategies
darkMode.subtitle: Multiple ways to handle dark mode in your application
darkMode.strategiesDescription: NUI provides three different strategies for managing dark mode, each suited to different use cases:

manual.title: Manual
manual.description: Full programmatic control. Dark mode is explicitly activated via ThemeService.
manual.feat1: Complete control in your application code
manual.feat3: Perfect for apps with a theme toggle UI

system.title: System
system.description: Automatically follows the user's operating system preference using the prefers-color-scheme media query.
system.feat1: Respects the OS dark mode setting
system.feat2: Updates automatically when the user changes their OS preference

auto.title: Auto
auto.description: Intelligent time-based switching. Dark mode activates during nighttime hours (8 PM - 6 AM local time).
auto.feat1: Activates dark mode from 8 PM to 6 AM
auto.feat2: Reduces eye strain during night use

demo.title: Try it
demo.description: The showcase is configured with the 'manual' strategy, so you can control dark mode programmatically:

customClass.title: Custom dark mode class
customClass.description: By default, NUI adds the 'dark-mode' class to <html> when dark mode is active. You can customize this class name:

cssVars.title: CSS Variable Explorer
cssVars.subtitle: Explore and search all 200+ CSS variables generated by the theming system
cssVars.noResults: No variables found matching your search

usage.title: How to use CSS variables
usage.description: You can use any NUI CSS variable in your component stylesheets:

customization.title: Advanced theme customization
customization.subtitle: Create your own theme presets with custom colors

intro.title: Why create custom themes?
intro.description: Custom themes allow you to adapt your brand identity, create unique user experiences, or offer multiple theme options to your users. NUI makes it easy with a type-safe configuration system.

customization.stepsTitle: Steps to create a custom theme

step1.title: Define your custom preset
step1.description: Create a ThemePreset object with colors for both light and dark modes. Each mode requires 8 semantic colors.

step2.title: Register your theme
step2.description: Add your custom preset to the NUI configuration in your app.config.ts or main.ts:

step3.title: Use your theme
step3.description: Your custom theme is now active! You can also switch between themes at runtime:

grayScale.title: Custom grayscale (Optional)
grayScale.description: You can also define a custom 10-step grayscale for even greater control over neutral colors. If omitted, NUI generates one automatically.

bestPractices.title: Best practices
bestPractices.practice1: Use hex colors for best compatibility and performance
bestPractices.practice2: Test in both light and dark modes to ensure readability and contrast
bestPractices.practice3: Keep semantic meaning consistent (e.g., 'danger' should always mean error/danger)
bestPractices.practice4: Use color contrast checkers to meet WCAG accessibility standards
bestPractices.practice5: Consider color blindness and do not rely solely on color to convey information

tip1.title: Multiple custom presets
tip1.description: You can register multiple custom presets and let users choose between them at runtime.

tip2.title: Preset inheritance
tip2.description: Start from an existing preset and override only specific colors to create variations.

tip3.title: Dynamic theme generation
tip3.description: Generate themes programmatically based on user input, brand colors or API data.

example.title: Complete example
example.description: Here is a complete example of a custom Forest theme with manual dark mode:

button.title: Button
button.subtitle: Versatile button component with multiple variants, sizes and states

basic.title: Basic button
basic.description: The simplest form of a button with default styles.

variants-colors.title: Variants & colors
variants-colors.description: Supports different variants and colors from the NUI system.
variants-colors.note: Combine color and variant to customize the button appearance to your needs.

shapes.title: Shapes
shapes.description: Control the button shape with rounded border options.
shapes.note: Shape options: 'rounded' for slightly rounded borders, 'pill' for fully rounded borders, and 'square' for straight borders.

raised.title: Raised buttons
raised.description: Add visual depth to your buttons with a subtle shadow.
raised.note: Raised buttons are ideal for primary actions you want to highlight in your interface. Use the raised attribute on your Button component.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Five size options available in NUI.
sizes.note: Sizes affect the button height, horizontal padding and font size. Use size with values 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg' and 'xl'.

icons.title: Icons
icons.description: Add icons to buttons at the start or end. Buttons with only an icon are rendered as circular icon buttons.
icons.note: Icon position: 'start' shows the icon before the text, 'end' shows it after. Icon-only buttons require the aria-label attribute for accessibility.

loading.title: Loading state
loading.description: Shows a loading spinner in three positions: start, end or center (which replaces the content).
loading.note: Loading positions: 'start' shows the spinner before the text, 'end' shows it after, 'center' completely replaces the content with a centered spinner. If the button only contains an icon, the content is also replaced by the spinner while loading.

width.title: Width
width.description: Control the button width: auto (default), full (100%) or fit (content-fitted).
width.note: Width options: 'auto' adapts to content with standard padding, 'full' stretches to 100% of the container, 'fit' uses the minimum necessary space.

disabled.title: Disabled state
disabled.description: Disabled buttons prevent user interaction and display a dimmed appearance.

events.title: Events
events.description: Handle click events with the onClick output. Supports button types: button, submit and reset.
events.consoleNote: Open DevTools (F12) and check the Console tab to see events logged when you click the buttons.

directive.title: Using directive
directive.description: Apply button styles to native HTML elements using the nuiButton directive.
directive.note: You can use the nuiButton directive on any native HTML element like <button>, <a> or <div> to apply NUI button styles and behaviors.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Input properties to configure the behavior and appearance of the button.

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the Button component.

computed.title: Computed Properties
computed.description: Reactive computed properties (Signals) of the component.

models.title: Models
models.description: Data models related to the Button component.

colors.title: Color variables
colors.description: CSS variables to customize colors by variant (solid, outline, ghost) and color (primary, secondary, etc.).

structure.title: Structural variables
structure.description: CSS variables for spacing, sizes, typography and visual effects.

actionMenu.title: Action Menu
actionMenu.subtitle: Dropdown menu component with support for static, dynamic items and submenus

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Simple action menu with an array of items. Each item can have a label, icon and action.
basic.note: Here is an example using both type='static' and type='dynamic'.
basic.consoleNote: Open DevTools (F12) and check the Console tab to see actions logged when you click on menu items.

separators.title: Separators
separators.description: Group related actions using visual separators.
separators.note: Use items with the separator: true property to add divider lines between groups of actions. You can add an optional label to the separator using the label property.

submenu.title: Submenus
submenu.description: Create nested menus using the children property. Submenus can be nested multiple levels deep.
submenu.note: Use the children property to define submenu items. Each child can have its own children for deeper nesting. Note: If a child has selected: true, the parent item will show a visual indicator (vertical line on the left) to facilitate navigation.

variants.title: Button variants
variants.description: The button supports different variants and colors from the NUI system. The variant affects the button, while the color affects both the button and its content.
variants.note: Combine color and variant to customize the appearance of the menu button to your needs.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Use any of the five NUI predefined sizes. The size affects the button, but the content remains the same.
sizes.note: Use the size input to adjust the menu button size and its content using xs, sm, md, lg and xl sizes.

label.title: Button content
label.description: Add text to the menu button. Can be combined with icons and supports full-width mode.
label.note: Use icon together with label to display both on the button. Use width='full' to make the button occupy the full available width. If you only use label without an icon, the button is rendered as a simple text button.

disabled.title: Disabled state
disabled.description: Disable individual menu items or the entire menu. Disabled items are displayed but not clickable.

withSubtitle.title: Item subtitle
withSubtitle.description: Add secondary text below the main label. Useful for showing additional information like emails, descriptions or shortcuts.

selected.title: Selected state
selected.description: Mark items as selected to show the current choice. A check icon appears automatically. Perfect for selectors, filters or settings.

icons.title: Descriptive icons
icons.description: Use meaningful icons and labels to communicate the purpose of each action. Icons help users quickly identify actions.
icons.note: Use relevant icons from the Remix Icon library to improve the visual understanding of menu actions.

template.title: Custom template
template.description: Completely customize the menu rendering using templates and directives.
template.note:
The #item template receives the full item as context (let-item). The menu-header and menu-footer directives enable a simple and clear syntax, avoiding unnecessary ng-templates.

Important: When using custom templates, you must handle the click event manually with (click)="handleAction(item)". The onItemAction event is only emitted automatically with default items.

inputs.title: Component inputs
inputs.description: Input properties that modify the behavior and appearance of the action menu.

outputs.title: Component outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the component that you can listen to in order to react to user interactions.

interface.title: ActionMenuItem interface
interface.description: Complete structure of a menu item with all available properties.

structure.title: Structural variables
structure.description: CSS variables that control menu dimensions, spacing, borders and shadows.

items.title: Item variables
items.description: CSS variables to customize the appearance of items, separators, shortcuts and internal elements.

colors.title: Color variables
colors.description: CSS variable pattern to customize menu item colors per state.
colors.note: Replace {color} with any NUI semantic color (primary, secondary, accent, success, info, warning, danger, neutral) to customize item appearance for different states.

styling.title: Styles & customization
styling.description: Customize the menu appearance via CSS variables. Control colors, spacing, typography and more.

avatar.title: Avatar
avatar.subtitle: Graphic component to represent users, entities or brands via images, initials or icons

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Display a simple avatar loading an external image. This is the most common form of user representation.

types.title: Content types & priority
types.description: The component intelligently decides what to display based on the available data. This logic ensures something meaningful is always shown.
types.note: Render priority: 1. Image (if loads) > 2. Initials > 3. Icon > 4. Fallback (Alt letter) > 5. Default icon.

variants.title: Shape variants
variants.description: The avatar supports three geometric shapes to adapt to different design contexts.
variants.note: Use the variant input with values 'circular', 'rounded' and 'square' to change the avatar shape.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Five predefined sizes that harmoniously scale the container, typography and icons.
sizes.note: Sizes affect the avatar dimension, font size for initials and icon size. Use size with values 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg' and 'xl'.

colors.title: Colors
colors.description: Customize the background and text color using the system palette. Ideal for differentiating users or states when there is no image.
colors.note: Use the color input with values like 'primary', 'secondary', 'accent', 'success', 'info', 'warning' and 'danger' to change the avatar colors. You can also use custom colors in rgb, hexadecimal or keyword format for full control over the avatar's appearance. If you use a custom color, the component will automatically calculate a contrasting text color to ensure readability of initials or icons.

customSize.title: Custom size
customSize.description: Define exact pixel dimensions when standard sizes don't fit. Font and icon sizes are automatically calculated proportionally.
customSize.note: The customSize input overrides the size input. The internal calculation maintains initials readability.

grouped.title: Grouped avatars
grouped.description: Display multiple avatars in a single component with overlapping and a display limit.
grouped.note: Use the nui-avatar-group component to group multiple avatars. Control the maximum visible number with max. Extra avatars are represented as a '+X' counter. You can use bordered to see a clear separation between each avatar.

error.title: Error handling (Fallback)
error.description: Automatic recovery system. If the image URL fails (404 error), the component instantly switches to showing initials or the fallback icon without breaking the layout.

inputs.title: Component inputs
inputs.description: Input properties that modify the behavior and appearance of the avatar.

outputs.title: Component outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the component that you can listen to in order to react to image load errors.

sizes.title: Size variables
sizes.description: CSS variables that control avatar dimensions, font sizes and icon sizes.

styles.title: Style variables
styles.description: CSS variables to customize borders, default colors and avatar typography.

styling.title: Styles & customization
styling.description: Customize the avatar appearance via CSS variables. Control sizes, colors, borders and more.

selectButton.title: Select Button
selectButton.subtitle: Grouped button set for single (Radio) or multiple (Checkbox) selection

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: The simplest case accepts an array of strings. By default, it acts as a single option selector (Radio).

modes.title: Selection modes
modes.description: Allows changing the button selection type between radiobutton and checkbox.
modes.note: Switch to mode='checkbox' to allow selecting multiple options at once. The returned value will be an Array.

complex.title: Complex objects
complex.description: Supports arrays of objects with custom properties for label, value and disabled state.
complex.note: Map properties of arbitrary objects using labelBy, valueBy, iconBy and disabledBy.

icons.title: Icons
icons.description: Supports icon-only buttons for a compact toolbar-style button bar.
icons.note: Remix Icon support. Use iconOnly='true' for a compact toolbar-style button bar. If it's an object, you can map the icon property using iconBy.

colors.title: Colors & variants
colors.description: Compatible with the full variant system (Solid, Outline, Ghost) and NUI colors.

segmented.title: Segmented variant (iOS style)
segmented.description: Visual style similar to iOS Segmented Control, special and in addition to NUI's basic variant types. Ideal for view toggles (e.g.: Month / Year, List / Map).
segmented.note: Use variant='segmented' to apply this unique variant.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Five predefined sizes that harmoniously scale the container, typography and icons.

width.title: Full width
width.description: Expand buttons to occupy all available space equally.
width.note: Use width='full' to make each button expand and occupy equal width within the container.

api.title: API & reference
api.description: Complete documentation of inputs, outputs and advanced usage of the Select Button component.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Component properties that control its behavior and appearance.

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the component during its lifecycle.

theming.title: Styles & customization
theming.description: Customize the Select Button appearance via CSS variables. Control spacing, colors, shadows and more.

general.title: General variables
general.description: CSS variables to customize the general style of the Select Button (default layout).

segmented.title: Segmented layout variables
segmented.description: CSS variables specific to the segmented style (iOS-like). Controls the container, selected/unselected states and hover effects.

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.subtitle: Component for selecting dates with multiple modes (day, week, range), sizes and width strategies

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Simple calendar for selecting a single date. Displays the current month and allows navigation between months and years.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Five size options (xs, sm, md, lg, xl) and a sixth auto option that adapts to the container. Sizes harmoniously scale all internal elements.
sizes.note: Sizes affect the width, cell height, padding and font size of the calendar. Use size with values 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg', 'xl' or 'auto'.

widths.title: Width strategies
widths.description: Two width strategies: compact (fixed width based on size) and full (occupies 100% of the container). Full mode uses Container Queries to adjust typography.
widths.note: Use width='compact' for a fixed-width calendar ideal for popovers. Use width='full' for a calendar that occupies all available space, ideal for forms or content areas.

week-selection.title: Week selection
week-selection.description: Special mode for selecting a full week (7 consecutive days). Useful for weekly planning.
week-selection.note: Set type='WEEK' to activate this mode. The calendar will highlight the full week on hover and return an object with the start and end dates of the week.

range-selection.title: Range selection
range-selection.description: Allows selecting a date range with start and end. Visually shows the intermediate dates of the range.
range-selection.note: Set type='RANGE' to activate this mode. The calendar will expect two clicks: one for start and one for end of the range. The output will return an object { start: Date, end: Date }.

presets.title: Quick range presets
presets.description: Add preset buttons for frequent ranges like 'Last week', 'Last month', 'Last year'. Allows including Remix Icons.
presets.note: This feature is only available when type='RANGE'. Provide an array of DateRangePreset objects in customPresets. Each preset has a label, range (with start/end) and optional icon.

time-picker.title: Time Picker integration
time-picker.description: Combine date selection with time selection. Supports start time, end time or both depending on the calendar type.
time-picker.note: Use showTimePicker='true' and configure timePickerMode with 'start', 'end' or 'both'. You can customize the time picker with timePickerConfig (steps, range, presets, etc.).

disabled-dates.title: Disabled dates
disabled-dates.description: Block specific dates from being selected. Useful for holidays, weekends or unavailable dates.
disabled-dates.note: Use disabledDates to specify an array of disabled dates. Disabled dates show a distinctive dimmed style and are not selectable.

min-max-dates.title: Minimum and maximum range
min-max-dates.description: Restrict the selectable date range using minimum and maximum values. Dates outside the range are disabled.
min-max-dates.note: Configure minDate and maxDate to set the boundaries. Users won't be able to navigate beyond these limits or select dates outside the range.

reactive-forms.title: Reactive forms
reactive-forms.description: Full integration with Angular reactive forms. Implements ControlValueAccessor for automatic synchronization.
reactive-forms.note: Use formControl or formControlName to integrate with Angular forms. The component automatically handles synchronization, validation and state. Supports all types (DAY, WEEK, RANGE) in forms.

dynamic-disabled-dates.title: Dynamically disabled dates
dynamic-disabled-dates.description: Disable dates based on complex business logic using a custom function. Overrides the static disabledDates array.
dynamic-disabled-dates.note: Use isDateEnabledFn to define dynamic enablement rules. The function receives a date and must return true if enabled or false if it should be disabled. Ideal for corporate calendars, booking systems or applications with complex availability logic.

date-status.title: Visual states (dateStatusFn)
date-status.description: Assign visual states to dates to indicate business information: availability, priority, events, etc.
date-status.note: Use dateStatusFn to assign one of four states to each date: 'success' (green), 'info' (blue), 'warning' (amber) or 'danger' (red). States are shown as a subtle color line at the top of the day. Return null for days with no special state.

selection-modes.title: Advanced selection modes
selection-modes.description: The calendar supports three specialized selection modes: normal day selection, month/year selection and year-only selection.
selection-modes.note: Each mode is optimized for a specific use case: type='day' for full date selection, type='month' for selecting month and year (ideal for monthly filters), and type='year' for selecting only the year (ideal for annual reports). The month and year modes start directly in the corresponding view and close automatically on selection if closeOnSelect is active.

multiple-selection.title: Multiple selection
multiple-selection.description: Allows selecting multiple dates, months or years with a single click. Ideal for selecting working days, holidays or distributed events.
multiple-selection.note: Activate multiple selection mode with selection="multiple" combined with type='day', type='month' or type='year'. Users can click on multiple elements to add/remove them from the selection (toggle). This mode never automatically closes the calendar, even if closeOnSelect is true, to allow continuous selection. The output returns an object with dates, months or years depending on the type.

week-numbers.title: ISO-8601 week numbers
week-numbers.description: Shows week numbers according to the ISO-8601 standard in a column to the left of the calendar. Useful for business, logistics and planning.
week-numbers.note: Activate week numbers with [showWeekNumbers]="true". Week numbers are clickable when type='week' or when type='day' and selection='multiple', allowing the selection of the full week with a single click. The ISO-8601 standard defines that the week starts on Monday and that week 1 of the year is the first week containing at least 4 days of the new year.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Input properties to configure the behavior, appearance and constraints of the calendar

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the calendar when the user selects a date or range

calendar-value.title: CalendarValue
calendar-value.description: Type union representing all possible calendar values depending on the selected type
calendar-value.note: The type of value returned depends on type: DAY returns Date | null, WEEK returns { start: Date, end: Date } | null, and RANGE returns { start: Date, end: Date } | null.

calendar-type.title: CalendarType
calendar-type.description: Enum that defines the calendar selection mode

calendar-width.title: CalendarWidth
calendar-width.description: Enum that defines the calendar width strategy

calendar-selection.title: CalendarSelection
calendar-selection.description: Enum that defines the calendar selection mode: single or multiple

view-mode.title: ViewMode
view-mode.description: Enum that defines the current view mode (days, months or years). Mainly for internal component use.
view-mode.note: This enum is used internally by the component to manage navigation between views. It does not need to be configured directly in most cases.

preset.title: DateRangePreset
preset.description: Interface for defining quick range presets with a label, date range and optional icon

smart-types.title: Smart Types
smart-types.description: Types and functions for advanced date validation and status logic. Allow applying complex business rules and dynamic visualizations.
smart-types.note: These types are fundamental for implementing advanced features: DateStatusFn assigns visual states (success, warning, danger, info) to dates based on business logic, while IsDateEnabledFn dynamically controls which dates are enabled or disabled. Both functions receive a date and allow implementing complex validations such as corporate calendars, booking systems or custom availability rules.

colors.title: Colors
colors.description: The calendar mainly uses global theme variables. Selected days, hover and active states use the primary color of the active theme.
colors.note: The component fully integrates with NUI's theming system. Primary, background, text and border colors are automatically inherited from the selected theme preset.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Size-specific CSS variables that control width, padding, spacing, cell sizes and typography. Five available size variants: xs, sm, md (default), lg and xl.

structure.title: Structure
structure.description: Structural CSS variables for controlling borders, opacities, animations and dimensions. Includes customization of tabs, days from other months, presets and focus states.

customization.title: Customization
customization.description: Examples of how to customize calendar styles in your application. You can override CSS variables globally or for specific instances.

paginator.title: Paginator
paginator.subtitle: Advanced component for data navigation with support for flexible layouts and infinite loading

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Standard configuration with numeric navigation. Supports two-way binding for page and size.

nav-display.title: Navigation display options
nav-display.description: Define how navigation buttons should be displayed: text, icons or both. Customize the appearance of navigation buttons to adapt them to your application's style.
nav-display.note: Use the navDisplay input with values 'text', 'icon' or 'both' to show only text, only icons or both in navigation buttons. Icons are based on the Remix Icon library and assigned automatically by button type (first page, previous page, next page, last page).

colors.title: Colors & variants
colors.description: Supports all NUI colors and variants (Solid, Outline, Ghost).
colors.note: Combine color and variant to customize the paginator appearance to your needs.

sizes.title: Page size options
sizes.description: Define a custom set of options for page size.
sizes.note: Using the size input you can offer users a specific set of page sizes to choose from.

layout.title: Custom layout
layout.description: The most powerful feature. Define exactly where each element goes (top, left, center, right, bottom) using a configuration object.
layout.note:
⚠️ Important for mobile: Complex layouts designed for desktop may overflow on small screens. Always provide an optimized mobileLayout that reorganizes elements vertically and prioritizes essential navigation. This prevents UX issues on mobile devices.

Areas system: When a layout is set, attributes like showPageSizeSelector, showFirstLast, showItemRange and showPageJump are ignored because the custom layout has full control over what to show and where.

infinite.title: Infinite loading
infinite.description: Transforms the paginator into a Load more button with integrated loading state. Ideal for mobile lists or feeds.
infinite.note: Activate infinite loading mode with infiniteConfig. The paginator becomes a button that loads more items when clicked. Use the onLoadMore output inside infiniteConfig to handle data loading logic.

api.title: API & reference
api.description: Complete documentation of inputs, outputs and interfaces of the Paginator component for advanced integration.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Input properties of the Paginator component

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the Paginator component

layout.title: PaginatorLayout
layout.description: Interface for custom layout: organize elements in areas (top, left, center, right, bottom)

infinite.title: InfiniteConfig
infinite.description: Interface for infinite scroll mode configuration

styling.title: Styles & customization
styling.description: Customize the paginator appearance via CSS variables. Control layout, buttons, page numbers and more.

layout.title: Layout
layout.description: CSS variables for the general paginator layout

buttons.title: Buttons
buttons.description: CSS variables for navigation buttons and page numbers

active.title: Active page
active.description: CSS variables for the active state of the current page

controls.title: Controls
controls.description: CSS variables for the size selector, item range display and page jump

infinite.title: Infinite scroll
infinite.description: CSS variables for infinite loading mode

popover.title: Popover
popover.subtitle: Interactive floating content component with support for text, templates and dynamic components. Unlike Tooltip, the popover can contain interactive elements such as buttons, forms or lists.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Simple popover with text content. The popover is shown on click (default behavior).

colors.title: Colors & variants
colors.description: Supports all NUI colors and variants (Solid, Outline, Ghost) to customize the popover appearance.
colors.note: Combine color and variant to adapt the popover style to your design.

positions.title: Positions
positions.description: Control the preferred popover position: top, bottom, left or right. The popover repositions itself automatically if there is not enough space.

events.title: Trigger events
events.description: Define how the popover is triggered: click (default), hover, focus or manual. Manual mode allows full programmatic control.
events.note: Using nuiPopoverEvent we have three modes: 'click' (show/hide on click), 'hover' (show on mouse over, hide on mouse out) and 'focus' (show on focus, hide on blur).

template.title: Custom template
template.description: Use Angular templates for rich interactive content. The template context provides a close function to close the popover.
template.note: The #popoverTemplate template receives a context with a close() function that you can use to close the popover from within the template.

component.title: Dynamic component
component.description: Render full Angular components inside the popover. Ideal for forms, lists or complex content.

delays.title: Show / hide delays
delays.description: Configure delays in milliseconds before showing or hiding the popover. Useful for improving user experience in hover mode.

customization.title: Customization
customization.description: Customize the popover appearance: no arrow, custom width, custom CSS classes or greater separation offset.
customization.note: You can use nuiPopoverShowArrow to hide/show the arrow, nuiPopoverMaxWidth to define a maximum width, nuiPopoverMinWidth to define a minimum width, nuiPopoverClass to add custom CSS classes, and nuiPopoverOffset to adjust the distance between the popover and the target element.

closeOptions.title: Close options
closeOptions.description: Control how the popover can be closed: click outside, Escape key, or allow multiple open popovers simultaneously.
closeOptions.note: Use nuiPopoverCloseOnClickOutside to close on outside click, nuiPopoverCloseOnEscape to close with Escape, and nuiPopoverAllowMultiple to allow multiple open popovers at once.

backdrop.title: Backdrop
backdrop.description: Add a dark background behind the popover to create a modal experience. You can control whether clicking the backdrop closes the popover.
backdrop.note: Use nuiPopoverBackdrop to enable/disable the backdrop and nuiBackdropClose to control whether clicking the backdrop closes the popover.

disabled.title: Disabled state
disabled.description: Dynamically control whether the popover is enabled or disabled.
disabled.note: When nuiPopoverDisabled is true, the popover will not show or respond to events.

config.title: PopoverConfig
config.description: Interface for global popover configuration via provideNUI({ popover: {...} })

context.title: PopoverContext
context.description: Interface of the context available in popover templates via let-close="close" and let-data="data"

tokens.title: Injection tokens
tokens.description: Tokens for injecting data and functions into dynamic popover components

spacing.title: Spacing & dimensions
spacing.description: CSS variables to control popover size, padding and spacing

typography.title: Typography
typography.description: CSS variables to control the popover content font

borders.title: Borders
borders.description: CSS variables to control popover borders and border radius

shadow.title: Shadow
shadow.description: CSS variable to control the popover shadow

zindex.title: Z-index
zindex.description: CSS variable to control the popover stacking order

animation.title: Animation
animation.description: CSS variables to control popover transitions and animations

toast.title: Toast
toast.subtitle: Non-intrusive temporary notifications for user feedback

basic.title: Basic toasts
basic.description: Show simple notifications with different types: success, error, warning and info.

withTitle.title: Toasts with title
withTitle.description: Add a title to give more context to the notification.

withActions.title: Toasts with actions
withActions.description: Include action buttons for the user to interact with the notification.

positions.title: Positions
positions.description: Control where toasts appear on screen: top-left, top-center, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-center, bottom-right.

iconTop.title: Icon at top
iconTop.description: Shows the toast icon centered at the top for a more prominent design.
iconTop.note: Use the iconPosition='top' property to center the icon at the top of the toast. This is especially useful for toasts with titles and actions, as it improves visual hierarchy and makes the icon more prominent.

icons.title: Custom icons
icons.description: You can show default icons based on the toast type or use custom icons. You can even hide the icon if desired.
icons.note: Use the icon property. If you use the value false the icon will be hidden. If you use a string you can specify a custom icon from Remix Icon. By default, icons are shown based on the toast type when icon is true.

duration.title: Duration
duration.description: Configure how long the toast remains visible before disappearing. Use 0 for toasts that don't close automatically.

loading.title: Loading toast
loading.description: Show a toast with a loading state that can be dynamically updated when the operation completes.

service.title: ToastService
service.description: Main service for showing toast notifications. Provides methods to create, update and close toasts.

config.title: ToastConfig
config.description: Per-toast configuration interface. Allows overriding the global configuration for specific toasts.

global.title: Global configuration
global.description: Global configuration for the toast system. Can be set during application initialization.

types.title: Types & interfaces
types.description: TypeScript type definitions for the toast system: ToastType, ToastPosition, ToastAction, ToastRef.

usage.title: Full usage example
usage.description: Complete example integrating ToastService in a component with different usage scenarios.

container.title: Container variables
container.description: CSS variables to customize the general toast container (background, border, shadow, spacing).

typography.title: Typography variables
typography.description: CSS variables to customize toast text (title, message, font size, weight).

icon.title: Icon variables
icon.description: CSS variables to customize the toast icon (size, color, spacing).

close.title: Close button variables
close.description: CSS variables to customize the close button (size, color, hover).

progress.title: Progress bar variables
progress.description: CSS variables to customize the automatic progress bar (color, height, animation).

types.title: Per-type variables
types.description: CSS variables specific to each toast type (success, error, warning, info, loading).

animations.title: Animation variables
animations.description: CSS variables to customize toast enter/exit animations.

examples.title: Customization example
examples.description: Complete example of toast customization with CSS variables to create a dark theme.

sidebar-panel.title: Sidebar Panel
sidebar-panel.subtitle: Sliding side panels with CDK Overlay for displaying dynamic content

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Open a side panel from the SidebarPanelService. The panel slides in from the edge of the screen with a smooth animation.

defaults.title: Default configuration
defaults.description: The panel will open using the configured global default values.
defaults.note: If no options are provided when opening the panel, the global default values defined in NUI configuration at provideNUI({ sidebarPanel: {...} }) will be used.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Allows defining the size at which the panel will open.
sizes.note: You can use predefined values like 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg', 'xl' and 'full'. On mobile, the 'full' size will be applied automatically to ensure an optimal experience on mobile devices.

positions.title: Positions
positions.description: Control from which side of the screen the panel slides in.
positions.note: Use the position property to define the side from which the panel will open using 'right', 'left', 'top' or 'bottom' as values.

html-content.title: Custom HTML content
html-content.description: Render arbitrary HTML content inside the panel using Angular templates.
html-content.note: Use the htmlContent property to pass an HTML string. You can generate the HTML dynamically or concatenate it with your data. This option does not require creating a full Angular component.

template-content.title: Custom template content (TemplateRef)
template-content.description: Use Angular templates with context data and functions without needing a full component. Ideal for reusable content with dynamic data.
template-content.note: Use the contentTemplate property to pass a TemplateRef and templateContext to pass data and functions. The template receives the context via let-varname variables. This allows interactivity without creating a component.

dynamic-component.title: Dynamic component
dynamic-component.description: Load any Angular component inside the panel and pass data via dependency injection.
dynamic-component.note: Use the data property to pass data to the dynamically loaded component. This data is injected using the SIDEBAR_PANEL_DATA token. If you also want to reference the panel itself from the loaded component, you can inject SIDEBAR_PANEL_REF.

dynamic-component-events.title: Dynamic component @Outputs events
dynamic-component-events.description: Listen to events emitted by the component loaded inside the panel to handle interactions and close the panel when necessary.
dynamic-component-events.note: You can subscribe to the outputs of the dynamically loaded component. Use panelRef.componentInstance to access the child component instance and subscribe to its @Output().

minimizable.title: Minimizable panel
minimizable.description: Panels can be minimized to a tab located at the side from which the Sidebar opens to free up screen space while preserving state, and can be restored when desired.
minimizable.note:
Use the minimizable attribute to make the Sidebar minimizable. You can also use the minimize() method of SidebarPanel to minimize the panel programmatically and restore() to restore it. If minimizable is enabled, the id attribute is required to identify the minimized panel. You can also check if the Sidebar is minimized via isMinimized.

Tab customization: You can customize the icon, add text, apply custom CSS or use a completely custom template via minimizedTabCustomization. For floating buttons with free positioning (e.g.: chat bottom-right), use standalone: true to render outside the grouped container and avoid positioning restrictions.

close-button.title: Close button
close-button.description: Control the visibility of the close button in the panel header.
close-button.note: Use the showCloseButton property to show or hide the close button in the panel header.

backdrop.title: Backdrop
backdrop.description: Add a dark background behind the panel to create a modal experience. Control whether clicking the backdrop closes the panel.
backdrop.note: Use the hasBackdrop property to enable/disable the backdrop (default true) and closeOnBackdropClick to control whether clicking the backdrop closes the panel.

custom-backdrop.title: Custom backdrop
custom-backdrop.description: Customize the backdrop with custom CSS classes. You can apply effects like blur, custom colors and transitions.
custom-backdrop.note: Use the backdropClass property to add custom CSS classes to the panel backdrop. You can add a single class or an array of classes.

multiple.title: Multiple panels
multiple.description: Open multiple panels simultaneously from different sides. Control stacking with z-index and close all at once.
multiple.note: Each open panel is independent and can have its own configuration. Use the closeAll() method of SidebarPanelService to close all open panels programmatically at once.

footer-actions.title: Footer actions
footer-actions.description: Add action buttons in the panel footer.
footer-actions.note: Use customButtons for simple actions or footerTemplate for fully customized layouts. You can combine multiple buttons with different NUI variants and colors.

child-footer-actions.title: Actions from child component
child-footer-actions.description: The child component can register its own footer actions using SidebarPanelActionsService.
child-footer-actions.note: This approach is ideal for forms where the child handles all the logic (validation, saving, states). The child component injects SidebarPanelActionsService and registers actions via register(). Actions can have reactive states (disabled, loading) and execute complex logic.

service.title: SidebarPanelService
service.description: Service methods for managing side panels

ref-properties.title: SidebarPanelRef (Properties)
ref-properties.description: Properties of the side panel reference

ref-methods.title: SidebarPanelRef (Methods)
ref-methods.description: Methods of the side panel reference for control and events

minimized-tab.title: MinimizedTabCustomization
minimized-tab.description: Customization of the tab when the panel is minimized

footer-actions.title: SidebarPanelAction
footer-actions.description: Definition of actions registered from child components for the panel footer

footer-actions-service.title: SidebarPanelActionsService
footer-actions-service.description: Service for child components to register actions in the panel footer

colors.title: Color variables
colors.description: CSS variables for controlling side panel colors: background, text, borders and overlay. These variables automatically adapt to the active theme's light/dark mode.

layout.title: Layout variables
layout.description: CSS variables for controlling side panel size and spacing: predefined size presets (xs to full) and header and content padding

typography.title: Typography variables
typography.description: CSS variables for controlling the typography of the side panel title

elevation.title: Elevation variables
elevation.description: CSS variables for controlling the shadow and z-index of the side panel and overlay

scrollbar.title: Scrollbar variables
scrollbar.description: CSS variables for controlling the scrollbar appearance of the side panel

examples.title: Customization examples
examples.description: SCSS examples for customizing side panel styles in your application

tooltip.title: Tooltip
tooltip.subtitle: Informational tooltips with support for text and custom templates

basic.title: Basic tooltips
basic.description: Show simple text tooltips on hover.
basic.note: You can hide the arrow with nuiTooltipShowArrow.

positions.title: Positions
positions.description: Control the tooltip position relative to the element: top (default), bottom, left, right.

events.title: Events
events.description: Define what event triggers the tooltip: hover (default), click, focus, or manual for programmatic control.

delays.title: Delays
delays.description: Configure delays before showing and hiding the tooltip.
delays.note: Use the nuiTooltipShowDelay and nuiTooltipHideDelay properties to adjust wait times.

templates.title: Custom templates
templates.description: Use Angular templates to show rich HTML content in the tooltip.

interactive.title: Interactive tooltips
interactive.description: Allows interacting with the tooltip content. Useful for including buttons or links.
interactive.note: Enable the nuiTooltipInteractive property to allow interaction inside the tooltip without it closing automatically when the cursor moves.

disabled.title: Disabled state
disabled.description: Dynamically control whether the tooltip is enabled or disabled.
disabled.note: Use the nuiTooltipDisabled property to activate or deactivate the tooltip as needed.

usage.title: Usage examples
usage.description: Complete examples of using the nuiTooltip directive in Angular components

container.title: Tooltip container
container.description: CSS variables for controlling the appearance of the tooltip container: colors, dimensions, padding, font, borders, shadow and z-index

arrow.title: Tooltip arrow
arrow.description: CSS variable for controlling the tooltip arrow size

animation.title: Animations
animation.description: CSS variables for controlling the duration and timing of tooltip enter and exit animations

examples.title: Customization examples
examples.description: SCSS examples for customizing tooltip styles in your application

timePicker.title: Time Picker
timePicker.subtitle: Component for selecting time with multiple modes, sizes and advanced configurations

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Simple time picker with default configuration. Select hours and minutes in 24-hour format.

modes.title: Time modes
modes.description: Five available modes: hours only (12h/24h), hours and minutes (12h/24h), and duration mode.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Five size options that harmoniously scale the component and its internal elements.
sizes.note: Sizes affect the height, padding and font size of the picker. Use size with values 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg' and 'xl'.

presets.title: Quick presets
presets.description: Add preset buttons for frequent times like 'Now', 'Breakfast', 'Lunch'. Can include custom icons.
presets.note: Provide an array of TimePreset objects in the config. Each preset has a label, time and optional icon. Supports Remix Icon icons.

steps.title: Hour and minute steps
steps.description: Control the increment intervals for hours and minutes to limit selectable options.
steps.note: Use hourStep and minuteStep in the config. For example, hourStep: 2 shows only even hours, and minuteStep: 15 shows quarters (00, 15, 30, 45).

timeRange.title: Time range
timeRange.description: Restrict the selectable time range using minimum and maximum values. Optionally shows a visual indicator.
timeRange.note: Configure minTime and maxTime in the config. Times outside this range are disabled. Use showRangeIndicator: true to visually highlight the valid range.

disabledTimes.title: Disabled times
disabledTimes.description: Block specific hours or minutes, preventing their selection. Useful for unavailable time slots.
disabledTimes.note: Use disabledHours and disabledMinutes in the config to specify arrays of disabled values. Disabled items show a distinctive dimmed style.

durationMode.title: Duration mode
durationMode.description: Special mode for selecting time intervals. Can display seconds and set a maximum duration.
durationMode.note: Set mode='DURATION' and configure with duration: { maxHours: 8, showSeconds: true } in the config. Useful for shift selection, time tracking, etc.

defaultStrategies.title: Default strategies
defaultStrategies.description: Control the initial time when no value is provided: now, smart (current + offset), empty or custom.
defaultStrategies.note: Use defaultStrategy with values 'now', 'smart', 'empty' or 'custom'. For 'smart', configure smartOffset in minutes. For 'custom', use defaultValue.

disabled.title: Disabled state
disabled.description: Disable the entire picker, preventing user interaction and displaying a dimmed appearance.
disabled.note: Use the disabled property to dynamically control whether the time picker is enabled or disabled.

reactiveForms.title: Reactive forms
reactiveForms.description: Full integration with Angular reactive forms. Implements ControlValueAccessor.
reactiveForms.note: Use formControl or formControlName to integrate with Angular forms. The component automatically handles synchronization, validation and state.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Input properties to configure the behavior, appearance and constraints of the time picker

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the time picker when the user interacts with it

config.title: TimePickerConfig
config.description: Configuration interface with all available options: steps, ranges, disabled times, presets and duration configuration

durationConfig.title: DurationConfig
durationConfig.description: Specific configuration for DURATION mode: hour limits, show seconds, steps and quick duration presets

models.title: Type definitions
models.description: Interfaces and type definitions for values, configuration and presets

methods.title: Methods
methods.description: The component implements ControlValueAccessor for seamless integration with Angular forms

colors.title: Colors
colors.description: CSS color variables generated by ThemeService for each semantic color. Each color generates 9 specific variables for different picker states: item backgrounds (normal, hover, selected), text colors, disabled states, accents and focus ring.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Size-specific CSS variables that control item heights, paddings and container dimensions. Five available size variants: xs, sm, md (default), lg and xl.

structure.title: Structure
structure.description: Structural CSS variables for controlling dimensions, spacing, typography and visual appearance. Includes separator style, scrollbar customization, borders, fonts and focus states.

customization.title: Customization
customization.description: Examples of how to customize time picker styles in your application. You can override CSS variables globally or for specific instances. Integration with ThemeService allows generating color variables for your custom semantic colors.

fabButton.title: FAB Button
fabButton.subtitle: Floating action button with radial layouts and multiple customization options

basic.title: Basic
basic.description: The simplest use of FAB Button: expands actions upward in a linear layout when the trigger is clicked.

directions.title: Directions
directions.description: Control the direction in which items expand with direction. Available values: up, down, left, right and the four diagonals.

layouts.title: Layouts
layouts.description: Define the spatial distribution of items with layout: linear (in line), semi-circle (semicircle), quarter-circle (quarter circle) and circle (full circle).

shapes.title: Shapes
shapes.description: Customize the border radius of the trigger and items with shape: circular, rounded or square.

colors.title: Colors
colors.description: Use any of the 8 semantic system colors with the color input.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Control the size of the trigger and items with size: xs, sm, md (default), lg and xl.

animation.title: Animations
animation.description: Choose how items enter and exit with animation: scale (default), fade or slide.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.description: Customize the trigger button with triggerIcon, triggerIconOpen (alternative icon when open), triggerLabel (Extended FAB pattern) and triggerBadge to show a counter.

loading.title: Loading state
loading.description: Shows a spinner on the trigger with [loading]="true". While active, the trigger does not open or close the dial.

openOn.title: Open mode
openOn.description: Control how the dial opens: openOn="click" (default) or openOn="hover" for a speed-dial activated on mouse hover. Keyboard navigation works the same in both modes.

itemDisplay.title: Item display
itemDisplay.description: itemDisplay="icon" shows only the icon with a tooltip on hover (default). itemDisplay="icon-text" shows the icon and the item's label property directly visible inside the button.

closeOptions.title: Close options
closeOptions.description: Control when the dial closes: [closeOnItemClick]="false" keeps the dial open after pressing an item (useful for multi-selection), [closeOnOutsideClick]="true" closes on outside click, [closeOnScroll]="true" closes on scroll.

backdrop.title: Backdrop
backdrop.description: Add a translucent layer behind the items with [backdrop]="true", ideal for highlighting actions over dense content.

templates.title: Custom templates
templates.description: Project your own content into the trigger with fabTrigger and into each item with fabItem. Both directives expose item context via let-item and the open state of the dial via let-open="isOpen".

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Input properties to configure the behavior, appearance and layout of the FAB Button.

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the FAB Button component to respond to user interactions.

computed.title: Computed Properties
computed.description: Reactive computed properties (Signals) that expose the resolved internal state of the component.

types.title: Types
types.description: TypeScript types that define the accepted values for FAB Button inputs.

item.title: FabButtonItem
item.description: Public interface that defines each menu action. All fields are optional: the only requirement is that the item is identifiable (via id) and has at least an icon or a label.

itemResolved.title: FabButtonItemResolved
itemResolved.description: Internal version of FabButtonItem enriched with four computed fields that the component injects as CSS variables into each <li>. It is the type received in the fabItem template context via let-item.

directives.title: Template directives
directives.description: Standalone directives that allow projecting custom templates into the trigger and each FAB Button item. They are imported and declared like any standalone Angular directive.

config.title: Global configuration (FabButtonConfig)
config.description: All global configuration properties available via provideNuiConfig({ config: { fabButton: { … } } }). These values are applied when the corresponding component input is not defined.

colors.title: Color variables
colors.description: CSS variables to customize colors by variant (solid, outline, ghost) and semantic color (primary, secondary, etc.).

structure.title: Structural variables
structure.description: CSS variables to customize the radial layout, animations, button sizes, backdrop, tooltips and focus ring.
